content:
I'm not able to generate rust-project.json from the out-of-tree-module repository. It says 

make: *** No rule to make target 'rust-analyzer'. Stop.

The command in running:

make -C ../../linux-rust M=$PWD rust-analyzer LLVM=1

Do you have https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230121052507.885734-1-varmavinaym@gmail.com/ applied? I'm not sure it has been upstreamed already. See also https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/rust-out-of-tree-module#rust-analyzer

Oh I didn't apply it, thanks! Also I was thinking of writing msi-ec as a rust external module. Is that possible? Or do I need to add something inside the kernel tree (eg for using acpi/battery.h or any other headers)
There are probably things with missing bindings that need to be added in-tree.
I am getting this error

danny@kernel:~/modules/rust-out-of-tree-module-main$ make -C ../../kernel M=$PWD make: Entering directory '/home/danny/kernel' warning: the compiler differs from the one used to build the kernel The kernel was built by: Ubuntu clang version 13.0.1-2ubuntu2.1 You are using: gcc (Ubuntu 11.3.0-1ubuntu1~22.04) 11.3.0 RUSTC [M] /home/danny/modules/rust-out-of-tree-module-main/rust_out_of_tree.o MODPOST /home/danny/modules/rust-out-of-tree-module-main/Module.symvers CC [M] /home/danny/modules/rust-out-of-tree-module-main/rust_out_of_tree.mod.o gcc: error: unrecognized argument to ‘-fsanitize=’ option: ‘array-bounds’ gcc: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-Qunused-arguments’ gcc: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-mretpoline-external-thunk’ gcc: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-Wformat-invalid-specifier’ gcc: error: unrecognized command-line option ‘-Wformat-insufficient-args’ make[1]: *** [scripts/Makefile.modfinal:29: /home/danny/modules/rust-out-of-tree-module-main/rust_out_of_tree.mod.o] Error 1 make: *** [Makefile:1938: modules] Error 2 make: Leaving directory '/home/danny/kernel'
try "LLVM=1 make ..."

I just started my journey in kernel Rust and I followed wedson's video to create the development environment.
I created the scull module as the mentorship tutorial states and everything works.
When I created a new .rs file in order to write a new module, the rust-analyzer got a warning file not included in module tree knowing that I added the module to both the Kconfig file and Makefile in the linux/samples/rust/ directory.
Any suggestions on how to solve this?
Did you run make rust-analyzer?
Yeah that just worked. Thank you very much @Trevor Gross . But if I may ask what does the target rust-analyzer do?
It generates the rust-project.json file using your current kernel config. Check make help :)
Miguel Ojeda has marked this topic as resolved.
When I compile rust-out-of-tree-module I got the error error: target file "./scripts/target.json" does not exist, anyone has idea?
Was the kernel compiled with rust support enabled? And have you made sure to not run make mrproper or anything else that removes generated files? make clean should be fine though afaik.

Yes, rust support is enabled.
Below is the config qemu-busybox-min.config, and I run with make LLVM=1 allnoconfig qemu-busybox-min.config rust.config, then run time sudo make LLVM=1 -j$(nproc) to compile kernel. After successful, I run make LLVM=1 KDIR=../linux to compile module

# Enable 64-bit for support Rust
CONFIG_64BIT=y
CONFIG_RUST=y

# Support vmlinux
CONFIG_HYPERVISOR_GUEST=y
CONFIG_PVH=y

# Enable to mount external kernel module
CONFIG_MODULES=y
CONFIG_MODULE_FORCE_LOAD=y
CONFIG_MODULE_UNLOAD=y
CONFIG_MODULE_FORCE_UNLOAD=y
CONFIG_MODULE_UNLOAD_TAINT_TRACKING=y

# Enable Rust samples
CONFIG_SAMPLES=y
CONFIG_SAMPLES_RUST=y
CONFIG_SAMPLE_RUST_MINIMAL=y
CONFIG_SAMPLE_RUST_PRINT=y
CONFIG_SAMPLE_RUST_HOSTPROGS=y

CONFIG_SMP=y
CONFIG_PRINTK=y
CONFIG_PRINTK_TIME=y

CONFIG_PCI=y

# We use an initramfs for busybox with elf binaries in it.
CONFIG_BLK_DEV_INITRD=y
CONFIG_RD_GZIP=y
CONFIG_BINFMT_ELF=y
CONFIG_BINFMT_SCRIPT=y

# This is for /dev file system.
CONFIG_DEVTMPFS=y

# Core networking (packet is for dhcp).
CONFIG_NET=y
CONFIG_PACKET=y
CONFIG_INET=y

# RTL8139 NIC support.
CONFIG_NETDEVICES=y
CONFIG_ETHERNET=y
CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_REALTEK=y
CONFIG_8139CP=y

# To get GDB symbols and script.
CONFIG_DEBUG_KERNEL=y
CONFIG_DEBUG_INFO_DWARF_TOOLCHAIN_DEFAULT=y
CONFIG_GDB_SCRIPTS=y

# For the power-down button (triggered by qemu's `system_powerdown` command).
CONFIG_INPUT=y
CONFIG_INPUT_EVDEV=y
CONFIG_INPUT_KEYBOARD=y
Are you compiling for x86_64 and does scripts/target.json exist in the linux checkout (or build dir if you use a separate build dir) or not?
Also I see you build the kernel as root, but the kernel module as regular user. Maybe a file permission issue?
No. I built it in docker container
and I try with sudo again, same error
And what is the answer to my previous questions?
I searched no target.json found
Are you building for x86_64 or arm64?

does scripts/target.json exist in the linux checkout No
build for x86_64
cause only x86_64 and um support rust
Ah, the arm64 support hasn't landed yet? Lost track of what has landed and what hasn't.

bjorn3 said:

Ah, the arm64 support hasn't landed yet? Lost track of what has landed and what hasn't.

I am not sure for arm64

For x86_64 scripts/target.json should be generated by the build system before any rust code is compiled. So if the kernel build succeeded with rust code compiled in, it should be available for kernel modules too.
I used same config to compile success last night on another mac with Ubuntu VM
By the way can you check if CONFIG_RUST is still enabled after the build is finished? Maybe rustc wasn't detected and it silently disabled rust support?

bjorn3 said:

For x86_64 scripts/target.json should be generated by the build system before any rust code is compiled. So if the kernel build succeeded with rust code compiled in, it should be available for kernel modules too.

OK, I will compile again to see if there has any error during building.

bjorn3 said:

By the way can you check if CONFIG_RUST is still enabled after the build is finished? Maybe rustc wasn't detected and it silently disabled rust support?

I checked it's removed. I have no answer with it
what does make rustavailable tell you?
I fixed the issue. it caused :rolling_on_the_floor_laughing: by I use sudo make LLVM -j$(nproc), after I remove sudo, it works.
yeah, don't use sudo unless you need to :)

but I blocked here

SeaBIOS (version 1.15.0-1)


iPXE (https://ipxe.org) 00:03.0 CA00 PCI2.10 PnP PMM+07F8B340+07ECB340 CA00



Booting from ROM..
I don't know why. cause I use same config on another Mac but use VM ubuntu to build, that is no issue.
I just use the same config from the rust branch.
Here is the error message: Booting from ROM..qemu-system-x86_64: Slirp: Failed to send packet, ret: -1

I copyed the building result files vmlinux and rootfs.img into VM which run Ubuntu, run with:

qemu-system-x86_64 \
    -kernel linux/vmlinux \
    -initrd busybox/_install/rootfs.img \
    -nographic \
    -nic user,model=rtl8139,hostfwd=tcp::5555-:23,hostfwd=tcp::5556-:8080

it works.
So why it can't run in docker container? I use the image ubuntn:latest.
Here is my config rust-qemu-busybox-min.config with run make LLVM=1 rust-qemu-busybox-min.config
and this is the final .config
I also run qemu-system-x86_64 on Mac directly, it also blocked the same as running in docker container.

Did it work for you with a non-Rust-enabled config and a mainline kernel?

In other words, did you confirm you can boot a "common" kernel in your setup first?
Yes . I tried today. I built with a mini config without rust support in docker with ubuntu, no errors during building, but it still can't work when run with qemu-system-x86_64
It's so strange. I compared the logs from VM and Docker, they have the same lines count, and I went through them with diff tool, no difference, only the order is not matched.

BleemIs42 said:

It's so strange. I compared the logs from VM and Docker, they have the same lines count, and I went through them with diff tool, no difference, only the order is not matched.

What are you comparing? Do you mean comparing Rust-enabled vs. Rust-disabled (i.e. you find strange that no Rust file was compiled) or working-config vs. non-working-config (i.e. you have another setup that actually works)?
I compared the logs of the Rust-disabled version which were built with Docker and VM, and all things seemed fine.
Here is the config file, rust is disabled. rust-qemu-busybox-min.config

Here are logs from docker and VM
log-docker.log
log-VM.log

Here are the images:
bzImage-x86 from VM, worked
bzImage-x86-docker from Docker, not worked.
rootfs.img

qemu-system-x86_64 \
    -kernel bzImage-x86 \
    -initrd rootfs.img \
    -nographic \
    -nic user,model=rtl8139,hostfwd=tcp::5555-:23,hostfwd=tcp::5556-:8080

Docker config:

colima status
INFO[0000] colima is running using macOS Virtualization.Framework
INFO[0000] arch: x86_64
INFO[0000] runtime: docker
INFO[0000] mountType: virtiofs

docker version

  GitCommit:

Docker file
Dockerfile

You said this worked on one Mac but not another? I’m assuming they both have Apple silicon?

What does uname -m show in your containers? I know that Docker has some support for aarch64, wonder if you could have two different Docker versions before/after support was enabled
Or just try applying the patches to enable aarch64 and see if it works, they are here https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20231020155056.3495121-1-Jamie.Cunliffe@arm.com/T/#u

Trevor Gross said:

You said this worked on one Mac but not another? I’m assuming they both have Apple silicon?

What does uname -m show in your containers? I know that Docker has some support for aarch64, wonder if you could have two different Docker versions before/after support was enabled

My machine is x86_64. I said I build with Docker and VM soft which install Ubuntu iso from its site, the building result of docker can't work, but the other one can work.

I'm a bit confused - as Miguel asked, what are the two or three things you are comparing?

It seems like you have:

  1. Building on your x64 machine (no docker) with no Rust, works fine
  2. Building on your x64 machine in Docker with no Rust, does not work
  3. Building on your x64 machine in Docker with Rust, does not work

...but that doesn't seem right?

Could you make an accurate list like that? Machine architecture, docker or local, rust or not, success or failure

OK.
Building on x64 machine in a VM(UTM, a VM soft)(install Ubuntn OS) with no-Rust or Rust-enable, all work fine.
Building on x64 machine in Docker(with image: ubuntu: latest) with no Rust or Rust-enable, all do not work. and no build erros find.
Mac: 2.6 GHz 6-Core Intel Core i7, OS 14.1.2

Oh, so the question is about building in docker more than about rust?

That is trickier. One thing is to make sure your checkout is on a case sensitive partition if using volumes, I know some files would get dropped otherwise (though this may have been improved recently)

Other than that it’s tough to know. Maybe this tutorial https://mgalgs.io/2021/03/23/how-to-build-a-custom-linux-kernel-for-qemu-using-docker.html or the questions here https://stackoverflow.com/questions/62455239/making-building-a-kernel-module-inside-a-docker-container would help
I went through the article, and I don't have the issues they mentioned.

BleemIs42 said:

Here are the images:
bzImage-x86 from VM, worked
bzImage-x86-docker from Docker, not worked.
rootfs.img

@Miguel Ojeda any idea ?
I uploaded my config, building logs and output image
